# SmartTec

> Battery-backed AI compute. SmartTec runs NVIDIA and Cerebras systems behind
> z1power LFP battery storage at an owned site in Mead, Oklahoma, US.
> Sub-10ms grid-to-battery failover by design. Phase 1 (30x NVIDIA B200,
> ~110 kW IT load) targets power-on Q4 2026.

## What SmartTec offers
- Reserved and on-demand NVIDIA GPU compute (H100/H200/B200 class)
- Cerebras CS-3 wafer-scale inference capacity
- Colocation with behind-the-meter battery-backed power
- AURA: in-house power/workload orchestration software

## Key facts
- Site: Mead, Oklahoma (US-70) — 30 acres, three 3,000 sqft buildings,
  3 MVA on-site transformer, owned outright (no interconnection queue)
- Connectivity: 100 Gbps symmetrical dedicated fiber (Dobson, signed quote)
- Power design: z1power LiFePO4 battery systems engineered by SmartTec with
  commercial-grade inverters/UPS; Oklahoma grid power ~$0.08/kWh
- Partner: z1power (z1power.com) — founder-owned LFP battery company,
  founded 2021 in Los Angeles by Syed Hussain, shipping US-wide since 2021
- Founders: Syed Hussain (CEO), Yasir Jahangir (COO)
- Status: pre-launch; design-partner slots open; power-on target Q4 2026

## Pages
- https://smarttec.dev/ : overview
- https://smarttec.dev/compute : GPU + Cerebras fleet
- https://smarttec.dev/power : battery-backed power design
- https://smarttec.dev/pricing : rates and reserved plans
- https://smarttec.dev/compare : honest comparison vs hyperscalers, neoclouds, marketplaces
- https://smarttec.dev/mead : the site story — owned land, 3 MVA transformer, power economics
- https://smarttec.dev/customers : who it serves + design-partner program
- https://smarttec.dev/blog : engineering articles on AI datacenter power
- https://smarttec.dev/contact : contact form (routes to team)
